title: bothriocline trifoliata
intro: Bothriocline trifoliata, also known as Thulasi or Wild Basil, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of India and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Bothriocline trifoliata include its ability to act as an antioxidant, protect against oxidative stress, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Bothriocline trifoliata can be made into teas, infusions, or essential oils, and are often used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
